senses.definition: describing brightly colored clothing made of reflective material in a way that is easily seen
(of a woman's clothing) having a deep V-shaped neckline that reveals a lot of the top and cleavage
very damaged or old in a way that has become unusable

examples.x: He donated his worn-out clothes to a local charity.
His worn-out backpack couldn't handle another school year.
She threw away her worn-out shoes and bought a new pair.
The chair's fabric was torn and worn-out from years of use.
The old, worn-out sofa needed to be replaced.
The worn-out tires on the car needed to be replaced before the long road trip.
The couch cushions were completely worn-out, offering little support.

senses.definition: (of clothes) not tight or fitting closely, often allowing freedom of movement

examples.x: After losing weight, his pants became loose and he needed a smaller size.
His pants were too loose after he lost weight.
His watch strap was too loose, so it kept sliding down his wrist.
The child's shoes were too loose, so they kept slipping off while running.
The loose shirt felt comfortable on a hot summer day.

senses.definition: (of clothes or shoes) fitting closely or firmly, especially in an uncomfortable way

examples.x: His shoes were too tight, and they gave him blisters.
She couldn't wait to take off her tight dress after the party.
The gloves were too tight on his hands, making it difficult to move his fingers.
The new jeans were so tight that she had trouble buttoning them.
The tight collar of his shirt made him feel uncomfortable.

senses.definition: (of a woman's clothing) showing most of the back or shoulders
(of women's clothing) revealing the neck and the upper part of the chest

examples.x: She wore a low-cut dress to the gala, attracting attention with its elegant design.
The low-cut blouse was perfect for the warm summer evening.
He felt uncomfortable when his date showed up in a low-cut top.
The low-cut neckline of her gown gave it a sophisticated, yet daring look.
She preferred a low-cut style for casual outings, feeling it was more comfortable.

senses.definition: (of clothes) tight-fitting in a way that shows the shape of the body
(of clothes) shabby, old and in poor condition

examples.x: The beggar wore ragged clothes and walked barefoot through the town.
His shirt was so ragged that it barely held together at the seams.
The children played outside in ragged outfits, unaffected by their condition.
She donated her ragged dresses to the textile recycling program.
The travelers emerged from the forest with ragged garments torn by branches.

senses.definition: (of a piece of clothing) displaying more of the wearer's body than usual
(of a piece of clothing) small and not covering much of the body
(of a woman's clothing) having a rounded and low-cut neckline
(particularly of clothes) so thin that light passes through and therefore one is able to see through it

examples.x: She wore a see-through blouse over a tank top to add a touch of elegance while maintaining modesty.
The fashion show featured a daring design with a see-through dress that highlighted the intricate patterns beneath.
The see-through fabric of the gown made it perfect for a high-fashion event where bold styles were celebrated.
He chose a see-through shirt for the beach party, which was both stylish and practical for staying cool.
The see-through panels in the dress created a modern look, blending sophistication with a hint of allure.

senses.definition: (of clothing) close-fitting, short, and exposing more of the body than typical
(of clothes) ready-made and provided in all sizes rather than suiting only a particular customer
very small, usually smaller than the standard or usual size

examples.x: The mini cupcakes were bite-sized and perfect for snacking.
She carried a mini umbrella in her purse for unexpected rain showers.
The mini fridge in the hotel room held just enough food for a quick snack.
He wore a mini backpack to carry his essentials while hiking.
He collected mini figurines as a hobby, displaying them on a shelf in his room.

senses.definition: describing a dress or skirt that falls below the knee but above the ankle
extending down to the ankles or floor
describing something that has been made by interlocking loops of yarn with knitting needles
(of a skirt, dress, etc.) being narrow at top and slightly wider at the bottom
a lifted collar that fits around the neck
(of clothes) short and revealing

examples.x: She felt uncomfortable in the brief dress, as it revealed more skin than she was used to showing.
The beachgoers lounged in their brief swimsuits, soaking up the sun on the hot sand.
He opted for brief shorts during the hot summer days to stay cool and comfortable.
The model strutted down the runway in a series of brief outfits that showcased her toned physique.
Despite the chilly weather, some daring individuals still wore brief attire to the outdoor concert, wanting to make a fashion statement.

senses.definition: (especially of clothes) having a shape that slightly widens toward the bottom
close-fitting and very tight
‌(of clothes) made with care and style and therefore highly priced
not damaged by the water or not letting water through
(of clothes) very tight
(of clothes) without any sleeves

examples.x: She opted for a sleeveless blouse to stay cool in the summer heat, pairing it with a flowing skirt for a casual yet chic look.
The sleeveless dress highlighted her toned arms, adding a touch of elegance to her ensemble.
He wore a sleeveless shirt to showcase the intricate tattoo on his upper arm, a conversation starter at the beach party.
Despite the chill in the air, she layered a sleeveless sweater over her blouse for a stylish and layered look.
The bride chose a sleeveless gown for her outdoor wedding, allowing her to move freely and comfortably as she danced the night away.

senses.definition: ‌(of clothing) tight or closely fitting to the body, emphasizing the contours of the wearer's body
(especially of a dress or bra) having no shoulder straps
(of a piece of clothing) having a neckline in the shape of the letter V

examples.x: She opted for a V-neck top to add a touch of elegance to her casual outfit.
The V-neck design elongates the neck, making it a flattering choice for many body types.
He wore a classic V-neck sweater over a collared shirt for a smart-casual look.
V-neck dresses are versatile, suitable for both formal events and everyday wear.
The V-neck style is popular in both men's and women's fashion, available in various materials and patterns.

senses.definition: a plain round neckline on a sweater or shirt that has no collar

examples.x: He chose a black crew neck for the casual outing.
The store’s collection includes a variety of crew necks in different colors.
Crew necks are comfortable options for everyday wear.
He prefers crew necks over V-necks for a classic look.
The crew neck he wore matched perfectly with his blazer.

senses.definition: an item of clothing with sleeves that extend from one's shoulders to one's elbows

examples.x: The store has a wide variety of short sleeves in different colors.
He packed several short sleeves for the vacation in a warm climate.
The short sleeves were perfect for the hot afternoon.
She bought a new short sleeve for the trip to the beach.
His short sleeve was stained after the meal.

senses.definition: a style of garment in which the sleeves extend to cover the arm from the shoulder to the wrist
clothing or garments that are designed for individuals who require sizes beyond the standard range, typically larger or smaller than the average sizes available

examples.x: The café served coffee in an extra small cup.
They ordered an extra small portion for the child.
The extra small suitcase fit perfectly in the overhead compartment.
His hands were extra small, making it hard to find gloves.
She bought an extra small T-shirt because the small was too big.

senses.definition: a size or measurement that is typically smaller than average, often used to describe clothing or other physical objects

examples.x: They found the small was out of stock in most sizes at the shop.

senses.definition: having a size that is not too big or too small, but rather in the middle

examples.x: They ordered a medium pizza to share among the group, neither too big nor too small.
The medium suitcase held enough clothes for a week-long trip.
The painting was of medium size, filling the space on the wall nicely.

senses.definition: a size or quantity that is greater than average or standard

examples.x: They ran out of larges, so I had to settle for an extra-large and have it altered.

senses.definition: (of a size) larger than large, often used for clothing, packaging, or other items

examples.x: He ordered an extra large coffee to start his day.
The shirt was too tight, so he exchanged it for an extra large.
She prefers extra large sweaters for a loose and comfortable fit.
The pizza came in an extra large size to feed the whole group.
He bought an extra large suitcase for his long vacation.

senses.definition: clothing or garments that are designed for individuals who require sizes larger than the standard range
items that are designed to fit a wide range of body sizes or shapes without the need for specific sizing
(of clothing) large, comfortable, and not fitting the body closely

examples.x: Loose-fitting clothes are more comfortable for exercising.
She wore a loose-fitting dress for the summer party.
He prefers loose-fitting shirts for casual outings.
The patient was advised to wear loose-fitting clothing after surgery.
The loose-fitting robe was perfect for lounging at home.

senses.definition: (of clothes) fitting tightly in a way that is not annoying and shows the shape of the body

examples.x: She wore a close-fitting dress to the gala that highlighted her figure.
The close-fitting gloves kept her hands warm without being bulky.
The close-fitting helmet ensured maximum safety while cycling.
He prefers close-fitting shirts that accentuate his physique.
The close-fitting suit made him look sharp and professional.

senses.definition: made in a single piece without separate parts
consisting of two separate pieces
being snug and covering the body closely
being dressed or equipped in a particular way, typically for a specific activity or purpose
not having any clothes on

examples.x: The baby giggled happily as she ran around undressed in the backyard.
He felt a bit self-conscious being undressed in front of the doctor during the examination.
The undressed statue in the museum drew both admiration and curiosity from the visitors.

senses.definition: (of a piece of clothing) close-fitting in a way that shows the outline of a woman's figure
not securely fixed or closed, often suggesting openness or looseness
(of a part of the body) not covered by any clothing

examples.x: She felt the cool breeze on her bare arms as she walked along the beach.
His bare chest glistened with sweat after a vigorous workout at the gym.
She blushed when she realized her bare feet were visible under the table at the fancy restaurant.
The hiker's bare legs were scratched and muddy after trekking through the wilderness.
He wore a sleeveless shirt that left his bare shoulders exposed to the sun.

senses.definition: a high, close-fitting collar that covers most of the neck, often folded over and sometimes extended into a roll-neck
a sleeve that ends between the elbow and wrist, covering approximately three-quarters of the arm's length
having decorative threads or strands hanging from the edge of a garment or material
having a uniform color without any patterns, gradients, or mixed shades

examples.x: She wore a solid blue dress to the party.
The walls were painted a solid white to make the room look bigger.
He prefers solid colors for his car, without any flashy designs.
The artist used a solid red background for the painting to highlight the figures.
The brand’s logo is usually printed in solid black for a sleek look.

senses.definition: neither short nor long, but falls in between in terms of length

examples.x: She opted for a mid-length dress for the semi-formal event, striking the perfect balance between casual and formal attire.
The hairstylist recommended a mid-length haircut that would be easy to manage and style.
The mid-length skirt was ideal for the mild weather, providing both comfort and style.
His mid-length beard gave him a rugged yet polished appearance.
The mid-length novel was just long enough to captivate the readers without becoming overwhelming.

senses.definition: worn-out or in poor condition, often indicating a lack of care or upkeep in its appearance

examples.x: His shabby jeans were torn at the knees and faded from years of wear.
The shabby coat hung on the rack, its seams unraveling and its fabric pilled.
She refused to wear the shabby sweater with holes and stains, preferring something more presentable.
They lived in a shabby apartment with peeling paint and broken furniture.
The shabby curtains in the living room were stained and tattered.
The shabby backpack was patched with duct tape, a testament to its long years of use.

senses.definition: describing the garments that have sleeves
(of an item of clothing) having sleeves that are long enough to reach one's wrist

examples.x: She wore a long-sleeved shirt to keep warm during the chilly evening.
For the outdoor event, he chose a long-sleeved dress shirt to maintain a polished look while protecting himself from the sun.
The long-sleeved pajamas were perfect for a cozy night in during the winter months.
He prefers long-sleeved tops for layering, especially in transitional weather.
The fashion designer introduced a new line of long-sleeved dresses that are both stylish and comfortable.

senses.definition: a clothing size category that is designed for people who are taller than average, typically above 6 feet or 183 centimeters in height
(of clothing) secured with buttons in a vertical row along the entire length of the garment
fine or intricate in texture, structure, or design
